beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, morning routine in kitchen.
beats.reasoning: Waking at 7am to drizzle; yesterday's settled mood carries into a calm start. Coffee ritual anchors the day before dealing with rain and SR-520 closure.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila via I-5 (avoiding SR-520 closure), steady rain.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; taking I-5 south adds buffer time. Rain steady but drivable. Tax season winding down means no urgency to rush.

beats.activity: Tax season work: client files, final Q2 prep.
beats.reasoning: Steady work block through the morning rain. Tax season winding down means focus without chaos. Wraps before the worst of midday restlessness.

beats.activity: Lunch at desk or nearby café, light break.
beats.reasoning: Rain still steady until 2pm; eating locally avoids driving in worst weather. Yesterday's restlessness lingers but today's introversion keeps her grounded.

beats.activity: Finish tax work, tidy desk for end of day.
beats.reasoning: Gentle wind-down at work; rain clearing by 2pm. No urgency; tax season is truly winding down.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle via I-5, rain clearing.
beats.reasoning: Rain ends by 2pm; I-5 now clear. Heading home via I-5 avoids any lingering SR-520 delays. Budget 60 min for the return leg.

beats.activity: Stop at Elliott Bay Book Company (Capitol Hill) for browsing.
beats.reasoning: Can't resist a bookstore. Weather has cleared; slight detour from home but feeds her introverted soul with quiet time among books.
Short drive back to Rainier Valley; evening ahead for home brewing and quiet leisure.

beats.activity: Home brewing: prep and early stages of new batch.
beats.reasoning: Equipment neglected yesterday; today's mild energy and cleared weather make this the perfect window. Introverted, hands-on, meditative work at home.
Staying in; quiet dinner at home after a day of commuting and work. Fuels evening leisure time.
Evening wind-down with favorite pastimes: chess and a book. Yesterday's settled mood continues; introverted comfort at home before sleep.
8 hours of sleep; ends at 6am on Wednesday. Restful night after a low-energy, rain-affected day that favored indoor time.

diary: Woke to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle—coffee tasted better than usual, maybe because I knew the commute would be a mess. SR-520 was closed, so I took I-5 down to Tukwila like everyone else. The rain hammered the windshield the whole way. Work was standard tax season chaos: client files, Q2 prep, the usual end-of-quarter scramble. Grabbed lunch at my desk, honestly can't remember what. The drive back was easier once the rain let up around noon. I ducked into Elliott Bay on Capitol Hill—needed that small moment of peace, just wandering the stacks. Picked up something on brewing technique I've been wanting. Got home and jumped into prepping a new batch. There's something meditative about it, measuring grains, checking temperatures. Simple dinner, then lost myself in chess puzzles until bed. The day felt balanced, somehow—work stress offset by small pockets of quiet.
thought_process: The closure and rain shaped everything; I'd planned to take SR-520 but switched to I-5 without much thought—just habit and practicality. Work was demanding, tax season always is, but I didn't let it bleed into the afternoon. The bookstore stop was deliberate; I needed to break the work-commute-work cycle. Brewing is my real escape, and I prioritized it over other things—felt right to prep that batch today. Chess and reading wound me down naturally. I think the grey weather actually helped; I wasn't restless or tempted to hike. Instead, I leaned into indoor rhythms: work, books, craft, quiet thinking. Fifty-one feels good when days have this kind of texture.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
